name: agent-pipeline-marketplace
description: Reusable Claude Code plugins: a multi-agent development pipeline and the Stanford STORM research method.

plugins.name: agent-pipeline
plugins.description: A suite of composable dev pipelines. Core: a Planner -> Coder -> Tester -> Reviewer -> Explainer feature pipeline, contract-driven (repo/task/diff/evidence) with stop rules and a hard review gate. Plus standalone lifecycle pipelines you run on their own or chain into /ship: /clarify (one-question-at-a-time brief), /debug (root-cause loop with a guard test), /security-review (read-only OWASP gate with a parseable verdict), /code-simplify (behaviour-preserving cleanup), /perf (measure-first optimization), and /grade-tests (mutation testing). Plus a cross-cutting context-economy discipline (terse, cache-friendly, lossless-evidence handoffs) adapted from Headroom's token-reduction ideas.

plugins.name: storm-research
plugins.description: Stanford STORM research method: 5 expert perspectives -> contradiction map -> synthesis -> peer review. Turns any topic into a sourced, reliability-scored briefing. Includes /research-feature, a repo-grounded variant that feeds the agent-pipeline /ship workflow.

plugins.name: pocock-local
plugins.description: Lean, CI-trusting companion to the Matt Pocock engineering skills in LOCAL-FILES mode. Mirrors the GitHub issues assigned to you into local markdown (read-only, snapshot + protected notes, worktree-safe), and runs a test-driven ship flow — scope with grilling, design failing tests, implement to green, confirm, open a PR — that leans on CI for the regression gate instead of re-implementing contracts, stop rules, or mutation grading. Routes stages to claude-fable-5 when available.
